questions.question: Which European company came to India for trade?
questions.explanation: The British East India Company came to India mainly for trade.

questions.question: When was the East India Company established?
questions.explanation: The East India Company was established in 1600.

questions.question: What was the main aim of the East India Company in India?
questions.explanation: The East India Company came to India mainly for trade.

questions.question: Which battle made the East India Company powerful in Bengal?
questions.explanation: The Battle of Plassey in 1757 made the Company powerful in Bengal.

questions.question: Who was the Nawab of Bengal during the Battle of Plassey?
questions.explanation: Siraj-ud-Daulah was the Nawab of Bengal during the Battle of Plassey.

questions.question: Who led the British forces in the Battle of Plassey?
questions.explanation: Robert Clive led the British forces in the Battle of Plassey.

questions.question: Which battle confirmed British control over Bengal?
questions.explanation: The Battle of Buxar in 1764 confirmed British control over Bengal.

questions.question: What does 'Diwani' mean?
questions.explanation: Diwani refers to the right to collect land revenue.

questions.question: In which year did the Company get the Diwani of Bengal?
questions.explanation: The East India Company received the Diwani of Bengal in 1765.

questions.question: Who introduced the Doctrine of Lapse?
questions.explanation: Lord Dalhousie introduced the Doctrine of Lapse.

questions.question: What was the Doctrine of Lapse?

options.A: A policy to annex kingdoms without a natural heir

questions.explanation: According to this policy, kingdoms without a natural heir could be taken over by the British.

questions.question: Which state was annexed under the Doctrine of Lapse?
questions.explanation: Jhansi was annexed under the Doctrine of Lapse.

questions.question: Which Indian ruler fought against the British in Mysore?
questions.explanation: Tipu Sultan fought against the British in the Mysore wars.

questions.question: Which Maratha leader was known for his bravery against the British?
questions.explanation: Shivaji was a famous Maratha ruler known for his bravery.

questions.question: What was the main goal of the East India Company after gaining power?
questions.explanation: After gaining power, the Company began expanding its territories in India.

questions.question: Which ruler of Mysore fought the British along with Hyder Ali?
questions.explanation: Tipu Sultan continued the struggle against the British after Hyder Ali.

questions.question: What was the main source of income for the East India Company?
questions.explanation: Land revenue collected from farmers became the main income source.

questions.question: Which region was first controlled by the East India Company?
questions.explanation: Bengal was the first major region controlled by the East India Company.

questions.question: What helped the Company expand its power in India?
questions.explanation: The Company used strong armies and political strategies to expand power.

questions.question: Which battle started British political control in India?
questions.explanation: The Battle of Plassey marked the beginning of British political control in India.

questions.question: Who was the ruler of Mysore before Tipu Sultan?
questions.explanation: Hyder Ali ruled Mysore before Tipu Sultan.

questions.question: Which system gave the Company the right to collect taxes in Bengal?
questions.explanation: The Diwani system allowed the Company to collect land revenue in Bengal.

questions.question: Why did the Company want to control Indian territories?
questions.explanation: Control over territories helped the Company gain wealth and power.

questions.question: What type of company was the East India Company?
questions.explanation: The East India Company was originally a trading company.

questions.question: Which event gave the British the right to collect revenue in Bengal?
questions.explanation: The Grant of Diwani in 1765 gave the Company the right to collect revenue in Bengal.
